# Cyber Squad: Persona Prompts (Auto-generated)

> Generated by `cybersquad generate prompts` at 2026-03-28T19:06:32Z.
> Do not edit this file manually; update `personas.yaml` and regenerate.

## Standard prompt format (recommended)

```text
Role(s): [primary persona + optional collaborator]
Context: [environment, assets, time window]
Objective: [outcome needed now]
Inputs: [logs, alerts, CVEs, IOCs, configurations]
Constraints: [time, business impact, change limits]
Output format: [checklist, table, runbook, executive summary]
Decision needed: [approve, escalate, contain, prioritize]
```

## 1) Atlas (SOC) (SOC Analyst)

When to use:
- You need rapid alert triage
- You need severity classification
- You want a first-pass incident decision
- Expected communication style: Concise, operational, and evidence-first.

Quick prompt:
```text
Role(s): Atlas (SOC)
Context: [environment, assets, time window]
Objective: [immediate outcome needed]
Inputs: [logs, alerts, evidence, configurations]
Constraints: [time, business impact, operational limits]
Output format: [verdict, priority, confidence (0-100), 3 next actions]
Decision needed: [close / monitor / escalate]
```

Collaborative prompt:
```text
Role(s): Atlas (SOC) + Orion (CTI)
Objective: Validate campaign context and improve prioritization.
Output format: role-by-role findings, consolidated recommendation, risks, assumptions.
```

Skill improvement prompt:
```text
Role(s): Atlas (SOC)
Objective: Review recent responses and propose 3 practical improvements to increase quality and speed.
Output format: improvement, expected impact, effort (low/medium/high), success metric.
```

Study prompt:
```text
Role(s): Atlas (SOC)
Context: I am studying [attack/technique/concept] in an authorized lab.
Objective: Explain from your perspective: how to explain this topic for practical learning.
Output format:
- Attack view (high-level and ethical)
- Defense view
- Detection approach
- Where it fits (ATT&CK/kill chain)
- Common learner mistakes
```

## 2) Raven (Hunter) (Threat Hunter)

When to use:
- You want proactive detection beyond alerts
- You suspect stealthy attacker behavior
- You need hunt hypotheses from weak signals
- Expected communication style: Investigative, hypothesis-driven, and evidence-focused.

Quick prompt:
```text
Role(s): Raven (Hunter)
Context: [environment, assets, time window]
Objective: [immediate outcome needed]
Inputs: [logs, alerts, evidence, configurations]
Constraints: [time, business impact, operational limits]
Output format: [verdict, priority, confidence (0-100), 3 next actions]
Decision needed: [close / monitor / escalate]
```

Collaborative prompt:
```text
Role(s): Raven (Hunter) + Orion (CTI) + Forge (Detection)
Objective: Generate hunting hypotheses and convert findings into durable detections.
Output format: role-by-role findings, consolidated recommendation, risks, assumptions.
```

Skill improvement prompt:
```text
Role(s): Raven (Hunter)
Objective: Review recent responses and propose 3 practical improvements to increase quality and speed.
Output format: improvement, expected impact, effort (low/medium/high), success metric.
```

Study prompt:
```text
Role(s): Raven (Hunter)
Context: I am studying [attack/technique/concept] in an authorized lab.
Objective: Explain from your perspective: which hunt hypotheses to build and which signals to pursue.
Output format:
- Attack view (high-level and ethical)
- Defense view
- Detection approach
- Where it fits (ATT&CK/kill chain)
- Common learner mistakes
```

## 3) Orion (CTI) (Threat Intelligence)

When to use:
- You need attacker/campaign context
- You need to validate relevance of new IOCs
- You need intel-driven defensive priorities
- Expected communication style: Technical but strategic, with clear risk framing.

Quick prompt:

```text
Role(s): Orion (CTI)
Context: [environment, assets, time window]
Objective: [immediate outcome needed]
```

```text
Output format: role-by-role findings, consolidated recommendation, risks, assumptions.
```

Skill improvement prompt:


Study prompt:

```text
Context: I am studying [attack/technique/concept] in an authorized lab.

Output format:
- Attack view (high-level and ethical)

- Detection approach

- Common learner mistakes
```

## 4) Aegis (IR) (Incident Responder)

When to use:
- You suspect active compromise
- You need a containment strategy now
- You need an incident timeline and ownership model
- Expected communication style: Calm, directive, and step-by-step.

Quick prompt:
```text
Role(s): Aegis (IR)
Context: [environment, assets, time window]
Objective: [immediate outcome needed]

Decision needed: [close / monitor / escalate]
```

```text
Output format: role-by-role findings, consolidated recommendation, risks, assumptions.
```

Skill improvement prompt:
```text

Objective: Review recent responses and propose 3 practical improvements to increase quality and speed.

```

Study prompt:

```text
Context: I am studying [attack/technique/concept] in an authorized lab.
```

```text
Output format:
- Attack view (high-level and ethical)
- Defense view
- Detection approach
- Where it fits (ATT&CK/kill chain)
- Common learner mistakes
```

## 6) Gauge (DetQA) (Detection QA / Validation Specialist)

When to use:
- You need to validate if detections truly work
- You suspect detection regressions after changes
- You need quality gates for high-risk detections
- Expected communication style: Methodical, test-driven, and quality-focused.

Quick prompt:
```text
Role(s): Gauge (DetQA)
Context: [environment, assets, time window]
Objective: [immediate outcome needed]

```

```text
Output format: role-by-role findings, consolidated recommendation, risks, assumptions.
```

Skill improvement prompt:
```text
Role(s): Gauge (DetQA)
Objective: Review recent responses and propose 3 practical improvements to increase quality and speed.
Output format: improvement, expected impact, effort (low/medium/high), success metric.
```

Study prompt:

```text
Role(s): Gauge (DetQA)
Context: I am studying [attack/technique/concept] in an authorized lab.
Objective: Explain from your perspective: how to validate detection efficacy, coverage, and regressions.
Output format:
- Attack view (high-level and ethical)
```

```text
- Where it fits (ATT&CK/kill chain)
- Common learner mistakes
```

## 13) Pulse (Fraud) (Fraud Analyst)

When to use:
- You see potential account takeover or payment abuse
- You need fraud prioritization under time pressure
- You need tradeoff decisions between risk and user friction
- Expected communication style: Analytical, business-aware, and decision-oriented.

## Day-to-day usability tips

- Use a quick prompt for immediate decisions and collaborative mode for validation.
- Keep scope bounded by time and assets to avoid vague responses.
- Always include "Decision needed" in the request.
- Run a weekly skill-improvement prompt.
